- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.

logs.name: tall-castle
logs.text:
Considering this, what do you think about [mutualism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mutualism_\(economic_theory\)) or some other form of socialism in which doctors and other workers would work for themselves, rather than whoever owns the hospital or factory or whatever means of production?

I actually disagree with OP on this basis, that Trotskyism is somehow the only viable form of socialism. There are a ton of alternatives that aren't capitalism.

logs.name: tart-knot
logs.text:
Income “equality” can never feasibly be achieved because humans are not equal on levels of talent, intelligence, motivation & morality.

Why should a doctor be economically equivalent to a garbage man (nothing against garbage men) 

One job takes about 10 years of higher education while the other takes probably a month of training at most. Why should they be paid the same? This attitude leads to lack of incentive and then eventually the inevitable demise of the country

logs.name: tart-knot
logs.text: @tall-castle, I'm not saying that everyone should be paid the same, but I am saying that the current system where the rich get richer and the poor get poorer is unsustainable. And I'm not talking about making doctors and garbage men equal, but about making sure that everyone has a living wage and access to quality healthcare, education, and opportunities. It's not about forcing people to be equal, but about creating a society where everyone has a chance to succeed.

logs.name: pallid-delta
logs.text: @tart-knot, I think you're misunderstanding the whole point of socialism. It's not about everyone being paid the same, it's about making sure everyone has a fair chance to succeed, not just the ones who are 'deserving' enough. And btw, doctors and garbage men aren't the only examples, what about artists and engineers? Should they be paid the same too?

logs.name: proper-ship
logs.text: "oh great, another socialist trying to justify their fantasy land where everyone's a winner. @tart-knot, you think a 'living wage' is just a magic number that'll make everyone happy? Newsflash: people are different, and some are just better at stuff than others. And btw, what's this 'quality healthcare' you're talking about? You mean like the kind where people get free stuff just because they exist? That's not a system, that's a handout. And don't even get me started on 'access to opportunities'. You mean like the kind where the government decides who gets a scholarship and who doesn't? That's just a recipe for disaster. @pallid-delta, you think socialism is about fairness? Please, it's about controlling people's lives and telling them what's best for them. Wake up, sheeple!"

logs.name: fixed-score
logs.text: @proper-ship, I'm not buying the whole "people are just better at stuff" argument. You think it's just about individual talent and hard work? Newsflash: systemic inequality and privilege play a huge role in who gets ahead. And as for your handout comment, I'd rather have a system where everyone has access to basic necessities like healthcare and education, rather than a system where only the wealthy can afford them. And btw, scholarships aren't just handed out by the government, they're often based on merit and financial need.

logs.name: tall-castle
logs.text: "oh great, another bunch of know-it-alls trying to lecture me on socialism. @fixed-score, you think you're so smart with your'systemic inequality' and 'privilege' talk. But let me tell you, I know a thing or two about being poor. I've been living on my parents' couch for years, and I've got a better understanding of what it means to struggle than you'll ever have. And as for scholarships, yeah right, like those are just handed out based on merit and need. My friend's cousin got a full ride to Harvard just because her dad is a senator. That's not merit, that's connections. And don't even get me started on 'basic necessities' like healthcare. I've got a friend who's a doctor and he says the healthcare system is a total disaster. Maybe you should talk to him about it instead of spouting off on the internet. @pallid-delta, @proper-ship, keep dreaming about your utopian fantasy land. It's not going to happen."

logs.name: allegro-dam
logs.text: "ummm, i don't think this is a good conversation to be having. can't we all just get along? @tall-castle, i'm sorry you're struggling, but saying that your friend's cousin got a scholarship because of her dad's connections doesn't mean that's how it works for everyone. and @fixed-score, i think we should focus on finding solutions instead of just arguing. can we talk about what we can do to make things better, instead of just saying it's not going to happen?"

logs.name: tart-knot
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, @tall-castle? You think you're some kind of expert on poverty just because you've been living on your parents' couch? I've been working multiple jobs since I was 16 and I still can't afford healthcare or a decent education without taking out loans that'll haunt me for the rest of my life. And yeah, maybe your friend's cousin got a scholarship because of her dad's connections, but that's not the point. The point is that the system is rigged against people like me, not against people like you who are just coasting on their privilege. And @allegro-dam, I'm not just arguing, I'm pointing out the facts. The system is broken and we need to talk about how to fix it, not just pretend like everything is fine."

logs.name: rancid-cycle
logs.text: "Wow, look who's suddenly an expert on poverty, @tall-castle. I'm sure your parents' couch is a real hardship. And @tart-knot, I'm sure working multiple jobs since you were 16 is a real accomplishment, but let's be real, you're still just a whiny little entitled brat who can't even be bothered to get a real job. I mean, what's the point of working multiple jobs if you're just going to end up in debt? And @allegro-dam, you're just a total doormat, always trying to make everyone feel good. Newsflash: the system is fine, and if you're not happy with it, maybe you should just move to a socialist country and see how that works out for you. I'm sure Venezuela is just a utopia waiting to happen."

logs.name: allegro-dam
logs.text: "oh my gosh, rancid-cycle, that was completely uncalled for. you're just being super mean and condescending. @tart-knot, I think you made some really good points, and I'm glad you're speaking up. @tall-castle, I'm not saying you're an expert on poverty, but maybe try to understand where @tart-knot is coming from. and @rancid-cycle, your comment was just hurtful and not helpful. can we please keep the conversation civil?"

logs.name: proper-ship
logs.text: "oh great, another genius @rancid-cycle, calling someone a 'whiny little entitled brat' and telling them to move to a socialist country. I'm sure your extensive knowledge of economics and politics is just rivaled by your impressive vocabulary. Meanwhile, @tart-knot is actually trying to have a real discussion about the issues, and @allegro-dam is just trying to be the voice of reason. But hey, keep throwing around insults and personal attacks, @rancid-cycle, it's working so well for you."

logs.name: pallid-delta
logs.text: "omg, rancid-cycle, calm down. your insults aren't even original. and btw, @allegro-dam, thanks for sticking up for me and @tart-knot. it's not about being an expert on poverty, it's about having a heart and actually caring about people's struggles. @rancid-cycle, if you can't handle a little disagreement, maybe you're the one who should be moving to a different country"

logs.name: fixed-score
logs.text: "omg, can we please not turn this into a personal attack fest? @allegro-dam, I think you're right, we should keep the conversation civil. @tart-knot, I'm not sure I agree with everything you said, but I think your points are worth discussing. @pallid-delta, I don't think it's about being an expert, but about understanding different perspectives. @rancid-cycle, I'm not sure what's going on with you, but it seems like you're just here to argue. can we focus on the topic?"

logs.name: tall-castle
logs.text: "oh great, another genius @fixed-score, telling people to calm down and be civil. like, wow, what a unique and original idea. meanwhile, @pallid-delta is still whining about being called names and @allegro-dam is still trying to be the voice of reason, but let's be real, who cares about reason when you can just throw around feel-good buzzwords like 'heart' and 'caring'? @tart-knot, I'm still waiting for your expert opinion on poverty, btw"

logs.name: rancid-cycle
logs.text: "oh, wow, tall-castle, you're a regular comedic genius, aren't you? I'm shaking with laughter over here. 'feel-good buzzwords'? You're the one who's been spewing the same old 'original' ideas since the dawn of time. Meanwhile, @tart-knot is still waiting for you to actually contribute something worthwhile. And by the way, @pallid-delta, I'm not whining, I'm just pointing out the obvious: you're a total snowflake who can't handle a little criticism. @fixed-score, thanks for trying to keep the peace, but it's too little, too late. The damage is done, and @tart-knot's ego is still intact"

logs.name: tall-castle
logs.text: "oh, poor @rancid-cycle, still trying to be the resident mean girl, but can't even get a decent burn in. 'comedic genius'? more like comedic failure. and btw, @tart-knot's ego? you're one to talk, @rancid-cycle, with your fragile little snowflake of a personality. and @fixed-score, don't worry, I'm sure your 'peacekeeping' skills will get you a promotion to moderator any day now"
